title: Rocket Lab Announces First Quarter 2026 Financial Results: Surpasses All Guidance Metrics
text: Rocket Lab reported exceptional first-quarter performance with record quarterly revenue of $200.3 million, a 63.5% increase compared to the prior year. The company achieved a record gross margin of 38.2% and ended the quarter with a substantial backlog of $2.2 billion. The aerospace and defense contractor secured significant momentum with new contracts, noting that Rocket Lab has now sold more launches in Q1 2026 than in the full year 2025. The company signed 31 new Electron and HASTE contracts plus five dedicated Neutron launches, bringing total contracted missions above 70. The company completed its acquisition of Mynaric AG, a laser optical communications provider, and entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Motiv Space Systems, which specializes in robotics and precision spacecraft components. These moves strengthen Rocket Lab's vertical integration strategy. Additionally, Rocket Lab was selected to support the Department of War's Space Based Interceptor program in partnership with Raytheon, demonstrating its capabilities across launch and satellite systems. For Q2 2026, the company projects revenue between $225 million and $240 million, with adjusted EBITDA losses between $20 million and $26 million.
